changes.before: Late sun warmed the harvested fields.
changes.after: Late sun over the harvested fields.

changes.before: Caer Darrow had spat them out of a school built over butcher's rooms and set them back among mud, fences, and people still thinking supper would happen if they got home before dark.
changes.after: They had come out of a school built over butcher's rooms and back into mud, fences, and people still thinking supper would happen if they got home before dark.

changes.before: Close enough to hear every word. Far enough to show the space.
changes.after: Sylvanas rode ahead and to the right by half a horse, close enough to hear every word.

changes.before: Jaina kept two stacks in her head because one would carry and the other would not.
changes.after: Jaina kept one stack ready for the road:

changes.before: The first stack could be spoken.
changes.after: She could say the first stack aloud.
Speaking the second stack would hurt.
Speaking the second stack would hurt.

changes.before: The word sat between them like a stone.
Nothing moved for three heartbeats except the horses.
Each time it had grown heavier.
changes.after: Each time keeping it back got harder.

changes.before: For a while they had nothing but distance.
Each northbound face made haste look smaller and more necessary.
That meant he had stopped wasting time on politeness.
Cyndia lowered her bow only when the lane had swallowed his noise.
changes.after: Cyndia lowered her bow only when his noise faded around the bend.
The ditches went dark first, then the fields.

changes.before: Muscles complained along her back where the sanctum's stillness had sat too long.
changes.after: Her back hurt from hours of stillness in the sanctum.

changes.before: None of them argued with the order buried in it.
She could spend what she had learned.
